candidates.stmt:
Successive Governments have cut public services, while the cost-of-living crisis deepens for working people, with energy, water and council tax bills all shooting up.

The Green Party is now the only progressive voice left in British politics; that’s why I’m standing for Mayor – I’m determined to drive meaningful change by:

• Ensuring development is carefully managed, prioritising the needs of the many over the few. 
• Delivering an integrated transport system, with more buses on more routes with simple and affordable fares. 
• Investing to end homelessness, fighting for tenants rights and campaigning for rent controls.
• Building more social housing because affordable housing simply isn’t. 
• Making sure housing developments include community facilities, and homes are properly insulated.
• Taking action to resolve the looming water crisis, and making more space for nature.
• Increasing support for small businesses, and growing the green economy.

Having directed the Cambridge office of a national construction company for 15 years, I understand business. I’m passionate about sustainability, and the importance of developing the skills of local people. Now retired, I work for social enterprises supporting the homeless and getting young people into construction.

Polls consistently report that most of us want to see the richest taxed and we want more government action on the climate crisis. But the major parties are not listening. The Greens are! If you want real hope and real change, VOTE GREEN on Thursday 1st May.

Whilst Cambridgeshire and Peterborough are largely rural areas, they are also home to dynamic and outward-looking bioscience and high-tech industries.

We've been held back for decades by lacklustre Conservative rule. But this year we have an opportunity to change that.

We have a vision for a fairer and greener Cambridgeshire and Peterborough.

🌳 I will set out a clear roadmap to get the whole county to zero-carbon. I will reverse the collapse in biodiversity through strong environmental policies, from tree planting to creating networks of natural spaces.

🚲 I will get people out of their cars, making walking, cycling and excellent public transport a realistic and attractive alternative for the majority.

🏠 I will build more homes that people can afford to rent and to buy, help renters get stable, long-term tenancies

🧑‍🎓 And I will get more people into good training and improve access to post-16 education

Cambridgeshire has traditionally been staunchly Tory, but over the last five years, we have been steadily transforming the county into a place of real Liberal Democrat strength.

In the last mayoral election in 2017, our candidate Rod Cantrill got a fantastic second place. We need a swing of just 7% to win this time.

In 2018, I led the campaign where we took control of the South Cambridgeshire Council. After three years of running the council, we are now the leading voice on the environment. We have been taking in vulnerable refugees and we started building our own houses. That’s the difference that electing Liberal Democrats can make.

This May we have a real chance to win in Cambridgeshire and Peterborough. We are challenging to win the county council, to win the mayoralty and to win the Police and Crime Commissioner.

I am representing the Green Party in the election on 7th May.  I believe in Hope, not Hate. I am concerned for the future of this planet because there is no planet B. I am inspired by Zack Polanski who speaks for people like me.

I’ve lived in Meldreth with my family for many years and work in Melbourn for a local technology company. For the last three years, I’ve served on Meldreth Parish Council, navigating local government to accelerate the delivery of local improvements. I want to see our area evolve in the best possible way, and to that end I hope to serve as your representative for Bassingbourn, Whaddon, Meldreth and Melbourn on Cambridgeshire County Council.

I have lived in the constituency most of my life and love it here. Like many people, I have become disillusioned with the lack of ideological diversity among our political class. All major parties support unsustainable levels of inward migration (while opposing home building locally), high levels of taxation, big government, pouring ever more money into a broken NHS, and net zero policies that will bankrupt the country. It's no wonder that our youth are disillusioned and leaving the country in droves. Vote Reform UK to speak up for sensible, pragmatic, pro-growth policies and for a renewed sense of optimism in Britain that we so badly need.

Judith Tustian Works for a Cambridgeshire based social enterprise and charity that gives work experience and help with developing social and life skills to people with learning disabilities or complex needs. Judith is a long term Cambridgeshire resident and knows many people across our lovely villages.

I have lived in the Caxton and Papworth ward for the last 30 years and feel lucky to live in such a beautiful, rural area where community spirit is strong. I also work nearby as a Nursery Teacher, and therefore am aware of many of the issues affecting people and families locally. I am an active member of the National Education Union and International Solidarity Officer for the Cambridgeshire District. I am passionate about celebrating the rich cultural diversity within our local communities, fighting racism, and bringing people together through shared humanity.

I have lived in Elsworth for 7 years and have a strong sense of the importance of community life and kindness to others.

I have been politically active all my life, and am particularly passionate on issues such as equality, anti-racism, Palestine liberation, LGBTQ+ rights, and animal welfare.

I believe we need to fight to uphold our right to protest, which is currently under attack and I regularly take part in national and local demonstrations and campaigns.

For the last two years, I have also co-organised a local, fortnightly Vigil for Palestine, speaking out against our government’s complicity in the genocide of the Palestinian people.

So much is going wrong in today’s money driven society and it is imperative that we start putting people and planet over profit.

I believe, that we must tax the super rich and divert money from warfare in order to generate the funding so desperately needed for our broken public services and to bring millions of children out of poverty.

Locally, it's easy to see the need for investment in our infrastructure, the roads are littered with dangerous potholes, affordable housing is critically needed, and cheap and frequent public transport is required.

Vote Green for real hope and real change.

I moved to Cottenham as a tenant and loved the village so much that I bought my first home here. I am involved in village life; for example, as a supporter of the multi-use games area behind the village hall; where I have been a member of the MUGA Committee in conjunction with the Parish Council. I believe that our council is too slow to address local issues, and I will push for faster and stronger action.

My plan for Cottenham is to:

- Fix the Potholes! Work closely with the County Council to highlight the multiple road improvements that need to be made across the villages! 
- Get the Bus Franchising back on track so this is more frequent in the areas that matter the most! 
- Tackle vandalism in the Village. It is heartbreaking to see areas like the playpark vandalised. I'd be pushing for more police on our streets.
- We have a lot of new build areas in Cottenham (such as the new Tillia Homes) and we need an infrastructure to keep up with the housing demands. I will be pushing for all new planning considerations to also contain infrastructure plans like new Schools/GPs/Shops to enable our village to thrive.

The Liberal Democrats have been far too complacent in Cottenham/Rampton for far too long and rely on election wins because of what they are not; and not what they are. I will hold them to account in Cottenham and on the wider council as it is so important to have diverse views. We don't want 45 Liberal Democrat Councillors with no accountability. Vote for me and I will hold their feet to the fire.

I'm Steve Bradshaw - a software developer living just outside of Cambridge, and I've been a member of the Green Party for over ten years.  For the usual reasons really, I've found the change in weather over my lifetime quite startling, and want to make sure that there's always a voice arguing for providing long term and sustainable solutions to this, and all issues, both local and national.

I share the Green Party's sense of fair play, that we should try to do things transparently and for the best of everyone, using data where possible and avoiding misinformation.

Outside of that I enjoy cricket, snooker, running, animals (obviously!), playing music and video games.

Lived in Fulbourn for over 20 years, a parish councillor since 2023, and stood previously for both county and district councils.

Wants to be elected to ensure that developers are forced to provide the infrastructure we need such as improved roads, expanded GP surgeries and play facilities sooner rather than later as our community continues to grow, as well as ensuring all our children have the best possible educational outcomes.

Before retirement, he was a senior partner in a firm of patent attorneys, setting up and running the firm’s highly successful Cambridge office.

I’m very proud and honoured to have been selected as the Liberal Democrat candidate for Northwest Cambridgeshire.  I’ve lived in Cambridgeshire for 35 years and I welcome this opportunity to serve my community at a national level.

As a Lib Dem District Councillor for 11 years I have a strong track record in delivering innovative community development projects including the award winning Gamlingay Eco Hub. I have  also been responsible for a range of rural business support and economic development initiatives and for promoting green energy projects which are profitable for communities.

‘My message to voters is clear, it is time that we brought truth and honesty back into politics’. Spin and alternative truth have had their day. It is because I care about people that I will always listen to them, I will always speak the truth and I will always keep my promises’.

I will fight to ensure that any Brexit deal is informed by facts and not fiction and is a fair deal for everyone. If the deal is going to cost families money they need to know exactly how much.

The other parties never cease to patronise the electorate with half truths and poor information. The electorate no longer wants a nanny state requiring us to ‘leave it up to them’. I believe that everyone deserves to know the facts and to be active players in the politics which shape their lives. I will trust the people of NW Cambridgeshire to know what is best for them and I hope that they will trust me to put that knowledge into practice.

Lived in Hardwick for the past five years. 

Regularly reports issues to both the County and District Councils such as littering and potholes, getting positive action for the community, and is eager to use the full powers of the role of County Councillor to support the local community. 

Particularly concerned about improving adult social care. 

Professionally, he works as an area organiser for UNISON the trade union, coordinating successful campaigns such as stopping the closure of the 999 call

With first-class Social Policy BSc and multiple years of working in local government in South Cambridgeshire, Laurence is well versed in broad and local policy issues- this includes a strong understanding of the Planning system. Born in the Rosie in Addenbrooke's, he is a local man who has spent the majority of his life in the region. A long term member of Little Shelford Cricket Club and now a coach and board member, he is involved in the local community. He has a wide range of passions and interests, most notably in climate & environment matters, local community & sports groups and supporting young people.

I live in Histon with my wife and two children, and I’ve fully immersed myself in village life. I’m passionate about improving the built environment, running an architectural consultancy in Cambridge that collaborates with local charities and organisations on decarbonisation and retrofit strategies.

As a founding co-director of the community not-for-profit Cambridge Retrofit Hub, I’m dedicated to decarbonising homes in Cambridgeshire, making them warmer, healthier, and more affordable to run. You can often find me at the Histon farmers' market on Saturday mornings at the HI Sustainability stall.

My involvement in local politics began when I worked to address flooding issues that affected me and other residents caused by the construction of the new primary school. I was appalled by the impact of the flooding on my neighbours, and since  County Council officers were not taking the necessary measures to resolve the problem, I coordinated with residents and engaged directly with the County Council. Eventually, I got them to acknowledge that the school’s construction was impacting the residents of Garden Walk and to commit to taking steps to rectify the issue.

As a keen runner, I recognise the importance of green spaces for our health and well-being. I understand the challenges of development and the need for facilities in our vibrant communities. Properly thought-out planning is essential for improving our environment while providing access to nature and maximising biodiversity.

As a councillor, I will build on Pippa Heylings’ strong legacy and work to advance policies that meet the needs of residents while ensuring our communities are green and sustainable. My understanding of residents’ needs, along with my experience in planning law and regulations and working with councils, will be invaluable.

I’m committed to standing up for local residents and being a strong champion for our area.

Part of the Libdem team for Histon, Impington and Orchard Park, we propose to make the South Cambridgeshire Green, Vibrant and Safe. In particular we wish to incorporate a sustainable policy framework for our village to protect the environment that attracts people here. My own particular priorities are to ensure that opportunities are provided to promote and expand our network of green spaces, that we facilitate the provision of housing that is accessible to our younger generation, and that we ensure that our lifestyle doesn't put that of future generations at risk.

I have a particular interest in maintaining the diversity of our community. Married to  an EU national, I have seen the worries and insecurity they have been subjected to and will press the local authority to make our foreign partners, in particular EU citizens, welcome in the District.

I am a qualified town planner with 20 years experience as an ecologist and countryside manager in a Welsh local authority, and 12 years as a project manager in a European network of local authorities interested in sustainable energy. This gives me a good understanding of how local authorities work and what they can achieve. I would be delighted to put that experience to the use of South Cambridgeshire.

I’m proud to be standing as a Green Party candidate, bringing years of local government experience and a strong commitment to Green values. Having lived in the village for over 19 years with my family, I care deeply about protecting what makes Histon, Impington and Orchard Park special.

I believe tackling climate change, protecting green spaces, and ensuring development is properly planned go hand in hand with supporting thriving local communities. I’m a keen cyclist and understand the importance of safe walking and cycling routes, better public transport and practical local climate action.

I serve as a Parish Councillor and as a trustee of HI Friends and the HI Pump Track, helping support opportunities for young people and strengthen community life. I’m also involved with the Salvation Army and run a small AV and IT business, so I have real-world experience of supporting local jobs and enterprise.

I believe in open, transparent local government and giving residents a genuine voice in decisions. As part of the Green Party team, I’m committed to working for a fairer, greener future for our community.

I’m standing to be a District Councillor because local issues have been ignored for too long. I have lived in the village for 14 years with my family and have developed a strong understanding of its needs, priorities, and challenges. I was recently elected as a Parish Councillor for Impington and will work closely with fellow councillors to ensure local issues are identified and addressed through collaborative, community-focused governance.

Years of cuts from both Conservative and Labour governments have driven up inequality, anti-social behaviour and left our infrastructure crumbling, while the Lib Dem run council has failed to respond effectively. New estates are being built without the services they need, and basic repairs to roads and paths are continually overlooked. I want to change that.

I’ve been a strong community voice before. During my Natural Sciences degree, I served as a student and department representative, worked as a student union accessibility officer, and supported staff strikes for fair pay and better working conditions.

As a scientist and astronomy enthusiast, I believe in evidence-based decision-making to improve services for everyone. We know, for example, that youth centres reduce crime and anti-social behaviour, and that communities thrive with more green spaces. As a disabled resident, I’m also committed to making our public spaces, facilities and services genuinely accessible.

I’m a software engineer living in Orchard Park. I’m an avid cyclist and a strong advocate for better public transport and safer, more accessible cycling infrastructure.

As a resident, I understand the unique issues Orchard Park faces, from unreliable transport links to the ongoing challenges around maintenance and community facilities. I’m committed to standing up for local people, whether that means challenging unfair and unreasonable service charges or finding new, sustainable ways to fund and protect the services our community relies on.

My approach is shaped by Green Party values: environmental responsibility and genuine community empowerment. That means pushing for practical, long‑term solutions that reduce inequality, cut pollution, and make our neighbourhood a healthier, more connected place to live. It also means ensuring residents have a real voice in local decisions, not just being consulted after the fact.

I want Orchard Park to be a place where people can move safely, breathe clean air, access the services they need, and feel proud of the community they’re part of, and I’m ready to work hard to make that happen.

I’m one of the youngest candidates in South Cambridgeshire and it is about time that younger people had a voice on the Council. It’s estimated that only 2% of councillors are under 30. I want to see more facilities for youngsters such as the pump track.

I’m disappointed that the Council allows developers to avoid Local Plan rules for 40% of affordable housing. I live near the site where 38 flats are being built next to the guided busway. There should be 15 affordable homes there, actually there’s none at all!

There’s Government funding to invest in better bus services and I’ll be speaking up for the number 8 to be back at every 20 minutes and going on to Addenbrookes, more bus stops especially in the Park Lane area, and proper bus shelters with lighting.

I live in the centre of Histon and am a community volunteer on litter picks and at the food bank.

One of the many things I like about living here is how easy it is to nip out to my local. However, I do want to see the Council use its licensing powers to balance fun things to do with residents’ needs so that people don’t suffer disturbed nights from pub gardens or hotel operations.

Like everyone else round here, I worry about dangerous potholes. As a councillor, I’d help pressurise the Lib Dem County Council to get a grip of its maintenance contract and make sure repairs are co-ordinated, and that they last.

I’m one of many who has had a bicycle stolen from Cambridge North - ranked as one of the worst for theft in the country. I will campaign for improved security.

I want to make Linton an even better place. My priorities are affordable housing and a more rational traffic system

 Hello, I’m Ernie Turkington. I’ve lived in our area for almost 20 years. Before retiring I was a headteacher at a special school for emotionally disturbed children. A very rewarding but at the same time a very challenging job. 

It taught me a lot about being patient and not being over-demanding. It also allowed me to hone my listening skills, then putting into action the outcomes of what I’d heard. 

As a Councillor one of my priorities will be to see how we can sort out our traffic system and I’ll be keen to hear your views on possible improvements.

I’m very concerned about the lack of affordable housing. It means that many young people simply can’t afford to live here where they grew up and are forced to move away. I back Labour’s plans to insist on a minimum of 40% affordable housing in new developments. And to use programmes such as Community Land Trusts and Rural Exception sites that enable building affordable housing for people with local connections.

Now I’m retired, I will have the time to put my energy into making these policies become reality.

Has lived in Linton for twenty years. As a former Headteacher, Senior Leader in education, and author on education issues, he is committed to making well-resourced comprehensive education work for all students across the County.

He wants to ensure that there is enough affordable housing for the coming generation and to encourage growth of public transport, while at the same time developing a positive sustainable environment that retains or improves our natural biodiversity.

Hello all, I am Silke and I love living in Longstanton. But there are many things that we really need to improve: public transport, cycle paths, better lights at night, more places to meet up with lovely neighbours. We need different voices in the district council to make this all happen. The Green Party can help! We stand for social justice and a better life for all of us.

Vote Green in your local election. You have two votes, use them to make the district council more diverse, we don’t need more Lib Dem’s, we need more democracy.

If you ask why I am not campaigning for what I stand for the answer is easy. We Greens are a team, we are people powered and not supported by money from billionaires. We have limited resources and this year we are all needed to help in Histon where we have a very good chance to get in.

But still you can vote for me or my daughter who stands alongside me. People and family power!

Green wishes
Silke

I live in Waterbeach with my wife and two children. I grew up here and have deep roots in our community. I want our local villages to thrive and to give people the best opportunity to live in a safe, pleasant and accessible environment.

My biggest frustration is how long it takes to get things done, which is why I am running to be your County Councillor - so that I can help change that.

My experience means I know how to get things done. I worked in local government for 14 years and now work for the public service union, UNISON. Last year I was elected to Waterbeach Parish Council and am using my skills to bring about change.   I want to see real and lasting improvements to our local villages. For the County Council, the ‘Waterbeach Division’ includes Milton, Landbeach, Horningsea, Fen Ditton and Chittering. I want all our villages to benefit from the new and improved infrastructure opportunities we are starting to see across our County, like the new bus franchising service and the funding boosts to local services like schools and GP surgeries.

I have three key things I want to do to.
1. We need better community facilities
South Cambs District Council has over £20m sitting in the bank that’s meant for social infrastructure. Our Parish Councils have money too. I have the drive to unlock this.
I am taking action to make sure our popular Waterbeach community library survives and thrives.
I’ll protect Milton Country Park and want speedy delivery of the promised North Park at Waterbeach new town.
I won’t rubber-stamp developer decisions. We need decent infrastructure that we can all be proud of.
2.Roads that are safe for all
​The County Council has an extra £10m from the new Government to fix the potholes. I’ll demand urgent action!
I am a longstanding campaigner for a 20mph zone for Waterbeach village and road safety on the A10. We deserve safer streets where people work, live and play.
I’m working with local campaigners to help bring improvements to the Humphries Way

I have lived in Waterbeach for over 20 years. I have been campaigning for many years to protect and improve Waterbeach  as development occurs in and around the village. For the past 3 years I have been leading the development of policies on highways in the Waterbeach Neigbourhood Plan including active travel routes, safe routes to schools and measures to cut rat running. I founded Waterbeach cycling campaign around 4 years ago to improve the conditions for people travelling by bike or on foot. When the Waterbeach New Town planning guidelines were being developed I campaigned to have them improved to make it safer and more enjoyable for people to walk and cycle to the shops and schools. Most recently, I campaigned against the proposed routes for the Waterbeach-Cambridge public transport schemes which (still) could result in demolition of homes or damage to the countryside west of Landbeach.

I am passionate about protecting our environment, fighting climate change and increasing biodiversity. For a number of years I worked with people to help them reduce their carbon footprint. More recently, I have organised repair cafes which are great community events matching repairers with people who want their stuff repaired.

If elected I would work with residents of the whole ward of Landbeach, Milton and Waterbeach to address issues such as housing, environment and those arising from new development such as increasing traffic.

I'm standing in Milton & Waterbeach to give residents the opportunity to vote Green.

The Green Party stands for real hope and real change. We want an economy that benefits the many, not just the wealthy few. We understand that climate change and the biodiversity crisis are the biggest challenges humanity faces, and that there is no environmental justice without social justice.

If elected, my first priority would be to listen to what residents need and represent them to the best of my ability on the District Council. I would push for affordable, reliable public transport, better active travel routes, and development that actually provides houses people can afford and want to live in.

I’m Elizabeth McWilliams, and I’ve lived in this ward for over 20 years  and am Chair of Landbeach Parish Council because I am driven to want to solve problems and I cannot imagine being other than a community activist. I’ve already done so much for our community, think how much more I could achieve if elected!

I always say I won’t take ‘no’ for an answer and I will always stand up against injustice to make life better for our residents.

I have been campaigning for over two years about the incredibly dangerous Humphries Way A10 junction. It’s me that set up a survey to get residents’ views to share with officers; set up a petition with over 600 signatures which I presented to the Council Council at a Full Council meeting; and worked with a Labour councillor from outside the division to bring a motion to that same meeting. Now officers are working on funding-gap solutions to get safety work commenced sooner. Without my work on this, we’d be waiting a lot longer.

I secured repairs for council for tenants in Landbeach, who couldn’t access their own driveways due to the terrible state of the lane they lived on – a pitiful state of affairs that had gone on for years, with our current Lib Dem County councillor (and previous Tory councillors), simply trotting out technical arguments about dormant charities owning the unadopted road and failing to help this group describing themselves as ‘the forgotten residents of Landbeach’. I’d had enough. I wrote to the Chief Executive among others and got the job done!

I successfully pushed the District Council to sign up to the White Ribbon campaign (engaging men and boys to end violence against women and girls) after an initial refusal by the Lib Dem administration. To do this I worked with an independent councillor, because I know when I need to work with people, cross-party, to get the right thing done for residents.

I want to see Waterbeach village retain its train station and have been communicating with the GCP and campaign

One thing we have learnt during the pandemic is that we all need a  helping hand every now and then., our communities are at their best when we work together.

I've lived in Waterbeach for 25 years. You may have seen my name on a ballot paper before. That's because I am a local person who stands up for the community's interests.

I've played prominent roles in community campaigns. This includes saving homes and allotments by seeing off proposals for the busway.

I passionately believe in a quality health services accessible to all. I want to see the Council co-operating with the elected mayor, Dr Nik Johnson, as he puts health at the heart of local policies.

I've worked in the NHS for 30 years. I've learned that in a large organisation, if you want something, you have to fight your corner. As a councillor I would be just as determined to get the best deal for our villages.

GP services should be in place ready as new residents move in. And we need affordable homes for local people and healthcare workers. The Council can and should be doing more to deliver.

I am standing again in 2021 and hoping to do very slightly better than in 2017, when your LibDem candidates came within a few dozen votes of displacing the incumbents.

Since then we were very grateful for your support in Sawston and Shelford when you returned 4 LibDem Councillors to help run the District Council.  We are getting positive feedback about good management, having made some key appointments at senior level. And people are very supportive of our Geren agenda which underpins all our policies.

Since that time however, things at a national level have gotten in a state due to the pandemic AND Brexit. Both of these have been exagerrated by their poor handling by the awful Boris Johnson Government.

We hope by voting for us at the County Council elections, to send a strong message to Central Government by electing a LibDem administration for Cambridgshire. 

Thank you. 
Brian

Has been a Sawston Parish Councillor since 2018, where he has seen first-hand the challenges the community faces. 

Having stood before as a County Councillor, he wants to assist the people of Shelford and Sawston with issues that matter to them, in particular affordable housing, regular, reliable public transport and quality state education. 

Works for Greater Anglia Railway in customer service and passenger assistance.

I'm delighted to be standing for election to South Cambridgeshire District Council, where as your hard-working local councillor for Shelford ward, I will bring fresh Green ideas to bear in the key areas of Planning, Environment, and Waste - the District Council's areas of responsibility.

I’ve lived and worked in Cambridgeshire for over 30 years, moved from central Cambridge ‘out to the villages’ when our son was little, and have seen him right through the local primary and secondary schools in this area. Always a very active member of our village community: from volunteering on the school PTA committee to being treasurer of the local tennis club, I’m well known for my attention to detail and for getting things done.

Previously an NHS manager for ten years, working in both acute care and then end of life care, I value our public sector and experienced first-hand the chaos and disruption following poor government decisions. I also worked for ten years as the one-woman support officer for local charity the Magog Trust, who own and manage Magog Down: those wonderful wildflower meadows and unrivalled views of the area will be familiar and very precious to many of my fellow residents.

I joined the Green Party because all the other political parties fail to adequately address the huge environmental and social challenges that we all face. Other parties' obsessive focus on purely economic ‘Growth’ at all costs must be challenged if we are to protect our environment and our waterways, and if we are to have any hope of genuinely enhancing our communities. The Greater Cambridge Partnership (GCP) states that its main purpose is ‘to support Growth': far from providing the improved public transport that we all crave, their busway plans threaten to concrete over our countryside and destroy our natural environment. We could have simply had much more frequent bus services on the existing roads, but the crazy way the GCP funding is provided means they can’t do that. Our local plann

I'm standing for election to South Cambridgeshire District Council because I believe our community deserves politics inspired by hope, councillors who will stand up for our environment, fight for genuinely better public transport, and put residents ahead of developers. The Green Party offers that vision: one where our villages and green spaces are protected, where public money is spent wisely, and where local democracy actually listens to the people it serves.

I've lived in the area since 2019 and am proud to call Great Shelford home. I believe local government works best when it's grounded in integrity, compassion, and evidence-based decision-making. That's the focus I want to bring to the District Council; improving planning decisions so they genuinely work for residents and the environment, and pushing for more effective use of public money, particularly to deliver the better public transport that South Cambridgeshire so badly needs.

As a tech entrepreneur, I know how to deliver reliable public services that actually work for people. I understand budgets, accountability, and the importance of getting things right. I've also volunteered with Cambridge Samaritans, supporting people through some of their most difficult moments - an experience that taught me the value of truly listening, and of showing up when it matters.

The District Council elections on 7th May 2026 are Shelford's opportunity to vote for what you believe in this time: Vote Green, for Real Hope and Real Change in your area.

I’m your County Councillor for Market. I have lived or worked here as a biochemist for over 40 years, including 8 years as a Governor of Parkside where my children went to school. I’m vice-chair of the County’s Environment and Green Investment Committee and the Audit and Accounts Committee. For the last 4 years I have been working hard for residents on issues that matter to you, including: the environment, transport, planning and housing. On May 1st I’m asking for your vote to continue this work for all the residents of Market!

I always work collaboratively and I’ll work constructively with other parties on the County Council, where Labour holds the balance of power. I’m a mother, working in the university, so education – a County Council responsibility – is important to me. For example, I’m concerned that some Newnham families are having problems accessing secondary schools for their children. If elected, I will work hard to tackle this issue.

Casework co-ordinator for her union, regularly advising and representing members on employment matters, including many cases with an equalities element. Also committed to improving social care, having been in the situation in which many find themselves, trying to find suitable help and care.

Lived in Whittlesford for 30 years, enthusiastically supporting various village clubs and events, and volunteering through a council-backed charity led to supporting friends and neighbours at planning, licensing and employment meetings.

Believes that those skills could be brought to our wider community if elected. Having seen the excellent support provided to her mother by a North-Eastern Labour council, Clare would like to see the same standard of care available here.

Worked as an engineer in the pharmaceutical, healthcare and education industries before retraining as a physics teacher working in a local state secondary school.
